name: Carrot-Leek Soup with Thyme
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 pounds Carrots, Sliced into 1/4 inch Thick Pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the stock and keep it warm over low heat.
2. Heat 1 Tbsp of olive oil and butter in a soup pot; add carrots, half teaspoon salt, and pinch of pepper. Saute over medium heat until carrots begin to glisten and release juices, about 10 minutes. Stir in 1 cup vegetable stock and scrape the bottom of the pot to loosen sugars.
3. Add another 3 cups of vegetable stock and potatoes; bring soup to gentle boil, then reduce heat and simmer covered for approximately 30 minutes, until carrots are very tender.
4. Add remaining vegetable stock and puree in blender or food processor until smooth. Return to pot and cook over low heat.
5. While soup cooks, cut leek into halves lengthwise, wash, and thinly slice. Heat 1 Tbsp olive oil in saute pan and add leeks, garlic, half teaspoon salt, dried thyme, and pinch of pepper. Cover and cook over medium heat until leeks are tender (about 5-7 minutes).
6. Add leeks to carrot puree and cook uncovered over low heat for another 30 minutes.
7. If using, stir in heavy cream and season with salt and a few pinches of pepper to taste. Garnish each serving with Gruyere cheese and sprinkle fresh thyme.
Makes 9 to 10 cups.
Tip: For enhanced flavor, add sweet potato to the soup.
Note: Use the most flavorful carrots for the best results.
